text: The fourth principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Business people and developers must work together daily throughout the project". Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: Information radiators help keep everyone informed and foster collaboration between business people and developers. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 50

choices.text: Minimum viable product (MVP)
Definition of ready
Backlog preparation
Information radiators

text: A new project has been initiated in ABC organization using an adaptive approach. The initial project requirements have been determined in the initial planning stages and prioritized by customer and product owner. What should be done to these requirements over the course of the project?
explanation: Agile project requirements should be revisited and reprioritized by customer and product owner over the course of the project. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 52.

choices.text: The requirements should be kept fixed unless a formal change request is raised and approved.
The requirements should be revisited and reprioritized over the course of the project.
The requirements should be kept unchanged to avoid scope creep.
The requirements should be prioritized by the project team as needed over the course of the project.

text: You are a project manager for a minor software project which is being developed using a waterfall approach. Your customer is requiring a minor change in project scope and expects you to do this without delays and additional costs. You believe that you have adequate authorization to make the decision by yourself, but you are not quite sure. What should be your next steps?
explanation: Whenever a change request may impact any of the project's baselines, a formal integrated change control process is always required. Every documented change needs to be approved or rejected by the Change Control Board (CCB) through the Perform Integrated Change Control process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: It is a minor change; you can approve it to increase customer satisfaction.
You can use the management reserve to accommodate for this minor change.
You can use the requested change to increase the profit from the contract.
Handle the request according to the integrated change control processes described in your project management plan.

text: A project manager for a construction project is meeting with the project team when they informed him that the political climate in their country is not stable and a curfew may be applied which will shorten the working hours by 2 hours daily. This may delay the project by one month. They hold a brainstorming session to try to minimize the impact of the curfew but they found no useful way. Which risk response strategy did they use?
explanation: No specific risk response strategy was selected, so the risk was accepted. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: Mitigate
Escalate
Transfer
Accept

text: Sam is a project manager for a project to design a car parking sensor. He has a limited scope definition and he wants to protect his company from financial risk. The design work will be outsourced to another company. Which type of contract should he choose for his project?
explanation: Here you can't choose FFP as you have limited scope. You should select CR contract as you have limited scope and of course CPFF is better than CPAF because in CPAF the buyer has higher risk than CPFF, because the buyer will pay more to the seller. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 472 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: Cost plus award fee (CPAF)
Time and Material (T&M)
Cost plus fixed fee (CPFF)
Firm Fixed price (FFP)

text: During product demonstration of the third iteration out of five, the product owner is not satisfied with the delivered product increment of this iteration and states that there is no value delivered. The team has completed all the user stories in the iteration backlog which were agreed during the iteration planning. What should the team facilitator do in this situation?
explanation: The project team has completed all the user stories in the iteration backlog, however no value is delivered. The team has nothing to do, this topic should be addressed by the team facilitator and the product owner. They should review the benefits management plan which describes how and when the benefits of the project will be delivered, and describes the mechanisms that should be in place to measure those benefits. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 33 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 236

choices.text: Ask the product owner to formally accept the product increment.
Review the benefits management plan with the product owner.
Escalate this topic to the project steering committee.
Ask the project team to work with the product owner to add more valuable features to the next iteration backlog.

text: You are managing a large tele-communication project. A senior manager has a great deal of authority regarding project decisions, however he does not have much interest in the project. In order to have a successful completion for your project, you will need continuous support from the senior manager in many project decisions. Which of the following engagement strategies should you use with the senior manager to keep him engaged?
explanation: According to the power/interest grid, the senior manager has high power and low interest. So the best engagement strategy is to keep him satisfied. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 512

choices.text: Manage him closely.
Keep him informed.
Keep him satisfied.
Monitor his actions.

text: You are the project manager for an electronic boards manufacturing project. This project is implemented in a country where both temperature and humidity are high. The boards you manufacture have some components that are sensitive to humidity. If the humidity exceeds 70% , it may damage some humidity sensitive components, which may delay the project by two weeks till you purchase other components, and also will cost the project about 2,000$. So, the humidity degree inside the lab which you use to manufacture electronic boards should not exceed 70%. You work with your project team to develop a response to deal with the humidity problem. What is called the 70% humidity degree?
explanation: Triggers are the warning signs that a risk has occurred or about to occur. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 417

choices.text: Risk Event
Threat
Risk Trigger
Issue

text: Julian is a assigned as a team member in a small product development project. The project has low budget and scheduled to be completed within 20 days. The project includes another team member and Albert, the project manager. They are all co-located in the same room. After they finalized the project management plan, Julian asked Albert why the project management plan doesn't include stakeholder engagement plan and communication management plan. How should Albert answer him?
explanation: The appropriate project management processes should be tailored to manage a project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 135

choices.text: Both communication management plan and stakeholder engagement plan are not needed.
A stakeholder engagement plan is necessary to avoid stakeholders resistance, while a communication management plan is not needed because the team is small.
Julian is right, a stakeholder engagement plan and a communication management plan must be added to the project management plan.
A communication management plan must be added to the project management plan, while stakeholder engagement plan is not necessary.

text: The project manager with the project team are completing WBS for the new construction project. There are more than 300 persons in the meeting representing 5 departments. Too many people are talking about later issues while earlier issues are being discussed. The session is hard to manage. What best describes the real problem?
explanation: The project sponsor is not responsible for creating WBS. Stakeholder Management Plan purpose is to detect the engagement levels of stakeholders. The communication management plan has not been created yet to be followed. The best answer is that the project manager here is managing a program. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 14 and The Standard for Project Management, Page 4

choices.text: The project manager didn’t create stakeholders engagement plan.
The project sponsor should be leading this kind of meetings.
The project manager is really managing a program.
The communications management plan isn’t being followed.

text: You are managing a multi-million dollars product development project which includes 350 stakeholders located in four countries. You are identifying stakeholders and analyzing their impact on your project to properly manage their engagement throughout the project life cycle. You noticed that there is a gap between current and desired engagement levels of a key stakeholder in the project. What is the best course of action in this situation?
explanation: The gap between current and desired for each stakeholder will direct the level of communications necessary to effectively engage the stakeholder. During planning for stakeholders engagement, you don't need to analyze why there is a gap between current and desired level of engagement, it is normal to find gaps according to the needs of the project. You are still at the beginning of the project and you have not done any effort yet to increase the level of engagement of stakeholders. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 12 and 13

choices.text: Increase the level of communications to effectively engage the stakeholder.
Submit a change request to the stakeholder engagement plan to effectively engage the stakeholder.
Change his location on the power/influence grid to high power/ high influence.
Try to understand why there is a gap between his current and desired level of engagement.

text: Patricia has been assigned to manage a large product development project. Midway through the project, she notices that many stakeholders ask her about the project status. She was asked from many stakeholders questions like "Are we on budget?", "Are we ahead or behind schedule?" and so on. She sends a weekly status report to a list of stakeholders. In light of this, what should she do?
explanation: There is a communication gap here. The project manager thinks that the project status report is sent to all relevant stakeholders. However, some stakeholders are missed. So the project manager should regularly assess the project communication effectiveness. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 389 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 14

choices.text: Assign a team member to address the stakeholders communication concerns.
Ask those stakeholders to check their emails.
Hold a weekly project status meeting with all stakeholders.
Update the list of recipients of the project status email.

text: You are managing a project with 34 team members and 7 key stakeholders. One team member identifies a problem with the current approach. To solve that problem you need to make changes to the project plan and its subsidiary plans. The project is time-sensitive and the customer previously indicated that any delays are unacceptable, and your team members tell you that the change may cause the team to miss at least one critical deadline. What is the best way to deal with this situation?
explanation: You must first evaluate the impact of change on all constraints. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Analyze the impact that the change will have on the work to be done, the schedule, and the budget.
Don’t make the change because any delays are unacceptable.
Ask the sponsor for guidance.
Make the change as soon as possible to the project plan and subsidiary plans, and ask the team to implement the change.

text: According to Barry Boehm model, the sweet spot is:
explanation: The Barry Boehm model helps identify the optimum amount of planning, sometimes called the sweet spot. The sweet spot is different for every project. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 170

choices.text: The point at which the project expenditures equals to the project returns.
The point at which the project team reaches the optimum amount of up front planning.
The point at which the project team reaches the required engagement level of stakeholders.
The point at which the project team achieves the project intended revenue.

text: Monica is a project manager on a large software project. The customer required a change to a major work package. She evaluates the impact of the change and informs the customer with the impact on project schedule and budget. But the customer won’t allow any change to the schedule and won’t pay anything more for implementing the change. Monica explains that the proposed change is outside the documented scope of the original work. The customer tells Monica that he doesn’t care what was in the original scope and that she needs to implement the change with no impact to schedule or budget. What conflict resolution technique is the customer using?
explanation: Here the customer is pushing his viewpoint at the expense of others; offering only win-lose solutions. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 169

choices.text: Confronting
Withdrawal
Smoothing
Forcing

text: Which of the following is an example of noise?
explanation: Noise includes any interference or barriers that might compromise the delivery of the message. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 373

choices.text: An email that's sent to the wrong person.
A project manager who doesn't notice an important clause in a contract.
Garbled text and smudges that make a fax of a photocopy hard to read.
When the team is not paying attention during a phone call.

text: In an adaptive project, the business analyst has written use cases before the sprint starts. This is known as:
explanation: BDD stands for Behavior Driven Development. BDD is an extension to TDD where instead of writing the test cases, we start by writing a behavior. Later, we develop the code which is required for our application to perform the behavior. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 56

choices.text: Conduct a spike
Test-Driven Development (TDD)
Behavior-Driven Development
Continuous Delivery

text: Alan is managing a hardware design project. The project SPI = 0.83 and CPI = 1. The customer is not happy with the project progress. He requested from Alan to return the project back on track. Alan suggested that they can remove a test from the project management plan. Removing this test will ensure that the project will be back again on track. The customer requested to perform all the work in the project management plan without adding any extra cost or cutting scope. Which of the following should Alan do?
explanation: As he can't cut scope and can't add extra cost, so the only possible method to shorten the project duration is to fast track the project. Monte Carlo and resource leveling are not schedule compression techniques. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Crash the project
Fast track the project
Level project resources
Perform Monte Carlo analysis

text: The project manager in an industrial firm is reporting the project performance during the bi-weekly progress evaluation meeting with the key stakeholders. The planned budget for completing the project to date is 10000000$, the project mangers' calculations shows that the actual work done by the project is 1.2 the planned cost, while the work done is 0.89 the planned work to be done to date, in light of these info the project is considered:
explanation: The CPI = 1.2 and SPI= 0.89 which means the project is under budget but behind schedule. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: On schedule and under budget
On schedule and over budget
Ahead of schedule and under budget
Behind schedule and under budget

text: XYZ organization has initiated a new software project. The new project will adopt an adaptive development approach. Resources are being carefully selected for this project because this project is for a new customer and expected to be in a long-term partnership with this customer. Which of the following types of resources will be suitable for this project?
explanation: Successful agile teams are made up of generalizing specialists, or “T-shaped” people. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 42

choices.text: People with deep specialization in one area.
Distributed work teams.
I-shaped
T-shaped

text: The organization is going to undertake a multi-phase IT project. The requirements of the first phase which is the design phase are well-known, however the organization is unfamiliar with the technology required to implement the those requirements. The second phase which is the deployment phase is similar to previous projects within the organization. Which development approach should be selected for this project?
explanation: In this scenario, the hybrid approach is the most appropriate as the first phase has high technology uncertainty and requirements are well-known, so adaptive approach is appropriate for the first phase and waterfall approach is appropriate for the second phase. Having one phase using adaptive approach and another one using waterfall approach is an example of using hybrid approach. Domain = Process.

choices.text: Waterfall
Incremental
Hybrid
Iterative

text: Which of the following best represents a sprint goal?
explanation: The sprint goal is the team's commitment for the functionality that they will deliver in the sprint. It must be quantified, so that it can be measured. They should produce a product increment of releasable quality. Domain = Business Environment.

choices.text: Deliver and deploy the final product into production.
Produce a software version of the application that includes the first six mentioned features in the product backlog and it should pass the acceptance test.
Produce a high quality product increment that includes as many features as possible from the mentioned features in the product backlog.
Produce a defect free product.

text: Product scope management involves the requirements that relate to the product of the project. Project scope management involves:
explanation: Project scope is the work performed to deliver a product, service, or result with the specified features and functions. The term “project scope” is sometimes viewed as including product scope. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 131

choices.text: The work the project manager will do to manage the project.
The project management efforts done by the project manager.
All work included in the scope management plan.
The work the project will do to deliver the product scope.

text: During iteration planning for an agile project, the team has committed to 50 story points for the coming iteration. However, the product owner wants the team to deliver five more story points which will deliver more value to the customer. The team is uncomfortable committing more as it hasn't understood the stories well. The agile coach is trying to solve the problem, what should she do?
explanation: Adding more details and acceptance criteria will help the team understand the stories better and make an informed decision about their commitment. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide

choices.text: She should inform the product owner that the five more story points will be added to the next iteration.
She should explain to the product owner that the team determines their commitment and he needs to accept what they have identified.
She should ask the product owner to review the stories and add more details and acceptance criteria to help the team better determine if they can deliver more stories.
She should ask the team to increase their velocity and if needed she can ask for more resources.

text: A new project has been initiated in ABC organization. The project is risky as it will be delivered in a new market for the organization and the project team doesn't have experience with the product of the project. Some of the project team members want to spend more time to plan up front to reduce uncertainty, oversights, and rework. While some other team members find that the longer the time spent planning, the longer it takes to get a return on investment and the more market share could be lost. Which of the following represents the optimum amount of planning required for this project?
explanation: By taking more time to plan up front, many projects can reduce uncertainty, oversights, and rework. However, the longer the time spent planning, the longer it takes to get a return on investment and the more market share could be lost. The Barry Boehm model helps identify the optimum amount of planning, sometimes called the sweet spot. The sweet spot is different for every project (not standard); therefore, there is no correct answer for the right amount of planning overall. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 170

choices.text: The team should spend more time in planning to reduce uncertainty.
The team should not spend much time in up front planning in order not to lose market share.
There is a standard sweet spot for this type of project to help identify the optimum amount of planning.
There is no correct answer for the right amount of planning overall.

text: An organization has started a project to produce a wireless headset for a customer. The project was planned to be completed within 6 months using an adaptive approach. Midway through the project the customer has added three more features due to changes in the market to remain competitive. The new added features will extend the project two more months. Prior the product release another competitor has released a similar headset with exactly the same features. The customer has requested adding four more features which has ended up with a product totally different than the agreed one at the beginning of the project. This project has high:
explanation: Volatility exists in an environment that is subject to rapid and unpredictable change. Volatility can occur when there are ongoing fluctuations in available skill sets or materials. Volatility usually impacts cost and schedule. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: Volatility
Uncertainty
Ambiguity
Complexity

text: Nicole is a project manager for a major software project. The project is very important for the customer. She and her team have finalized the project management plan which resulted in a schedule that is too long. She needs to compress the schedule when she analyzed the network diagram, she found that it can’t change. She has extra personnel resources. What is the best thing to do?
explanation: Resource leveling will increase the project duration, Monte Carlo analysis doesn’t relate to increasing or decreasing project durations, fast tracking can’t be applied in this case as she can’t change the network diagram, thus the activities that were originally planned sequentially can’t be changed to be in parallel, crashing the project is the best answer because she has extra available human resources. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Perform Monte Carlo analysis
Level the resources
Crash the project
Fast track the project

text: Which of the following activities is closely related to Agile Principle 2: "Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer’s competitive advantage"?
explanation: As expressed in Agile Manifesto Principle 2, staying flexible to meet the customer’s changing business needs is fundamental to agile, and the backlog is one of the key ways that agile teams ensure this adaptability. The backlog is always evolving, and it needs to be kept continually updated with the latest requirements and new information. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 52.

choices.text: Limiting WIP
Grooming the backlog
Frequent verification and validation
Minimizing waste

text: Which of the following can be a result of not engaging stakeholders effectively?
explanation: Not engaging stakeholders effectively can lead to product recalls or loss of organizational or project reputation. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 12

choices.text: Stakeholder commitment
Project is completed on budget
Project is completed on time
Product recalls

text: You are a project manager for an industrial project. Your project is being audited by the company’s quality assurance department. They have found that your team has implemented the process inefficiently, which lead to defects in your product. What’s the next thing that should happen on your project?
explanation: They are now involved in the Manage Quality process. One of the outputs of this process is Change Request. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 296

choices.text: Add the problem to the risk register and develop a response plan.
Suggest corrective actions and submit them to the change control board.
Add the results of the audit to the lessons learned.
You meet with the manager of the quality assurance department to find a solution of the problem.

text: Adam recently joined a leading software organization to initiate a project management office. The PMO should have control on all the projects running in the organization and the project managers should report directly to the PMO because the new PMO will be accountable for project success in the organization. Which type of PMO is being initiated here?
explanation: Directive PMOs take control of the projects by directly managing the projects. Project managers are assigned by and report to the PMO. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 48

choices.text: Supportive
Controlling
Directive
Proactive

text: A project manager for a hardware design project and his team are using cause and effect diagram, trying to determine how various factors might have contributed to project problems. The project manager is involved in which part of the quality management process?
explanation: The key word here is (might have contributed) this means that the problem already happened, so they are involved in the Control Quality process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 304

choices.text: Total Quality Management
Control Quality
Manage Quality
Plan Quality Management

text: During the iteration planning meeting, the development team has selected 50 story points to be implemented in the next iteration according to the team's velocity. The product owner has requested from them to add two more story points to the next iteration. What should the development team do?
explanation: During iteration planning, the development team has the final say on the amount of work that can be accomplished in the iteration. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Reject the product owner request.
Add the two story points to the next iteration.
Replace them by another two story points.
Increase the team velocity to be 52 story points per iteration.

text: During executing a project to produce a new pharmaceutical compound, an industry standard has been issued by the government which takes an immediate effect. The new standard may require audits on the project to ensure project compliance. The audit may be performed at any time throught the project. The audit might have a negative impact on the project like re-work, auditors might find inconsistencies and disrupting the project management plan. What is the best way to handle these audits?
explanation: Incorporating agile methods with traditional methods would make the overall project management approach hybrid. In this situation, the project team could use the agile technique of a spike. Spikes are useful for learning and may be used in circumstances such as: estimation, acceptance criteria definition, addressing a risk, and understanding the flow of a user’s action through the product. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 56

choices.text: As unplanned spike.
Ask the sponsor to postpone these audits.
Inform stakeholders that the project may not achieve its objectives.
Accommodate audit impacts from management reserve.

text: Dan is managing a large engineering project containing 450 stakeholders located in 3 countries. He is classifying project stakeholders according to their level of authority, need for immediate attention and whether their involvement is appropriate. Which stakeholder classification model is he using?
explanation: Salience model describes classes of stakeholders based on assessments of their power (level of authority or ability to influence the outcomes of the project), urgency (need for immediate attention, either time-constrained or relating to the stakeholders’ high stake in the outcome), and legitimacy (their involvement is appropriate). Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 513 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 171

choices.text: Power/interest grid
Power/influence grid
Impact/influence grid
Salience model

text: Which of the following is the correct order from highest level to lowest level?
explanation: Product vision drives product roadmap which drives release plans. Release plan establishes the iterations. Iteration plans schedules feature development. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 216

choices.text: Product roadmap - Feature - Release plan - Iteration plan
Feature - Product roadmap - Release plan - Iteration plan
Product roadmap - Iteration plan - Release plan - Feature
Product roadmap - Release plan - Iteration plan - Feature

text: Carol is the project manager for a new engineering project. Some of the project work will be contracted to another company. She is discussing with the procurement manager about the appropriate contract type for the contracted work. Which of the following is the most important aspect to consider in determining the contract type?
explanation: What best determines contract type is the completeness of procurement statement of work. If SOW is detailed enough you can select FP contracts which has least risks for the buyer. But if the procurement SOW is incomplete, you can select CR or T&M contracts and accept higher risks for the buyer. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 471–472 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: The completeness of the procurement statement of work.
The cost of the contracted work.
The team experience of dealing with which types of contracts.
The available lessons learned in the organization about working with different contract types.

text: You are an agile practitioner working on a product development project using agile approach. The organization you are working on has a long history of managing projects using waterfall approach and has recently adopted agile approach for some projects. The development team for your project has been holding product demos at the end of each iteration and you are pleased with their progress. However, you hear that the project sponsor has told another stakeholder that "nothing is getting done using that agile approach." What should you do?
explanation: During iteration review (product demo) the team demonstrates all completed work items at the end of the iteration. It is better for the sponsor to see the completed work himself instead of sending reports. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 55

choices.text: Ask the product owner to send performance reports to the sponsor.
Invite the sponsor to attend the team's next product demo.
Educate the sponsor about Agile's incremental approach.
Ask the team to start showing working product that has business value rather than prototypes.

text: Barbara is managing a large industrial project. Recently, her company received many complaints due to defects found by the customer in the first deliverable. The customer is not satisfied at all with the first project deliverable. The project sponsor is very angry and told her that the company may lose the contract with the customer if they find defects in the next deliverables. In order to reduce sponsor's stress, she told him that they will compare the product’s quality standards to those of another comparable successful products to identify best practices and increase the probability of customer acceptance. Which tool is she using?
explanation: Benchmarking is the comparison of actual or planned practices of your project to those of other projects to identify best practices and improve performance. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 284

choices.text: Matrix diagrams
Logical data model
Benchmarking
Mind mapping

text: A risk owner will be responsible for:
explanation: A risk owner will be responsible for planning an appropriate risk response and for reporting progress on managing the risk, will be allocated to each individual project risk as part of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 426

choices.text: Determining the actual probability and impact for individual project risks.
Planning an appropriate risk response and for reporting progress on managing the risk.
Determining the needed contingency reserve for cost or schedule.
Reassessment of current risks and closing of outdated risks.

text: Antony is managing a large product development project. He and his team have finalized the first round of risk identification with project key stakeholders. They identified hundreds of individual project risks, recommended potential risk owners and risk responses. They also identified sources of overall project risk. Where should sources of overall project risk be documented?
explanation: On completion of the Identify Risks process, information in the risk report may include sources of overall project risk, indicating which are the most important drivers of overall project risk exposure. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 418 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 190

choices.text: Risk Report
Risk Register
Risk Management Plan
Risk Breakdown Structure

text: The organization's CEO is highly concerned about one of the agile projects being implemented in his organization. He wants to know the progress of the project. What is the best demonstration of progress that the team can come up with?
explanation: The second value of Agile Manifesto states that we value working software over comprehensive documentation. The seventh principle also states that Working software is the primary measure of progress. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Burndown Chart
Working product
Information Radiators
Project Status Report

text: James is a project team member. He is attending a project weekly status meeting. The project manager is explaining the project status to all the attendees in the meeting. James is moving his head to express his understanding to what the project manager said. What did James do?
explanation: Acknowledge means upon receipt of a message, the receiver may signal (acknowledge) receipt of the message, but this does not necessarily mean agreement with or comprehension of the message—merely that it has been received. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 373

choices.text: Decoding
Encoding
Feedback
Acknowledge

text: Those who act responsibly to carry out activities with integrity, care, and trustworthiness while maintaining compliance with internal and external guidelines are known as:
explanation: Stewards act responsibly to carry out activities with integrity, care, and trustworthiness while maintaining compliance with internal and external guidelines. They demonstrate a broad commitment to financial, social, and environmental impacts of the projects they support. Domain = People. The Standard for Project Management, Page 24

choices.text: Stewards
Servant Leaders
Project Managers
Scrum Masters

text: Which of the following is the correct pairing of leadership styles and team phases in Tuckman model of team formation and development progress?
explanation: When the team is in the forming stage, the role of the team leader is to directly help with project activities and present a clear, tactical picture of what needs to be done. So the directing leadership style is more directive than supportive. As the team passes into the storming phase, there will generally be plenty of disagreement, open conflict, and harsh dialogue. During this stage, the leader needs to assume the coaching style to help team members resolve conflicts without damaging relationships. When the team reaches the norming phase, the members have successfully created rules (team norms) to help govern themselves. Performing teams are autonomous, empowered, self-managing, and self-policing.

choices.text: Directing and Forming, Coaching and Storming, Supporting and Norming, Delegating and Performing
Directing and Forming, Supporting and Storming, Coaching and Norming, Delegating and Performing
Directing and Forming, Coaching and Storming, Delegating and Norming, Supporting and Performing
Supporting and Forming, Coaching and Storming, Directing and Norming, Delegating and Performing

text: The project team is working on a large software project. They adopt an adaptive approach. After the first iteration review, the customer has accepted the product increment. The team wants to track the accepted deliverables over time for the next iterations. Which of the following artifacts can they use?
explanation: Throughput chart shows the accepted deliverables over time. A throughput chart may be shown as a scatter diagram or a bar chart. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 190

choices.text: Burndown chart
Story map
Cumulative flow diagram (CFD)
Throughput chart

text: WBS is a good way that helps you to manage a project effectively. In WBS you should breakdown your work into smaller and more manageable pieces. Which of the following doesn’t describe how far to decompose the project work?
explanation: The lowest level of WBS components, which are called work packages can be done by more than one person. Other choices are all true about work packages. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 158 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 84

choices.text: Until it can’t be logically subdivided further.
Until it can be realistically estimated.
Until it can be done by one person.
Until it has a meaningful conclusion.

text: Simplicity - the art of maximizing the work not done - is essential. What does this principle mean?
explanation: Principle 10 of the agile manifesto states that Simplicity—the art of maximizing the amount of work not done—is essential which means that we should look for simpler solutions to avoid overengineering and prevent unnecessary work by keeping the essential requirements only. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 53

choices.text: Agile teams consist of 9–12 persons only.
Boiling down the requirements to their essential elements only.
Working in short iterations automatically leads to simplicity.
Agile approaches focus on team empowerment.

text: John is managing a product development project. He and his team decide to outsource part of the project work to another company. They have completed the procurement statement of work. Technical details of the outsourced work are set precisely in addition to the required quality levels. The procurement team want to select the contract type. Which contract type is the most appropriate in this situation?
explanation: Since the procurement statement of work is precisely defined and future cost risks should be avoided, then the most suitable contract type is FFP Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 471 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: Time and material (T & M)
Fixed price- economic price adjustment (FP-EPA)
Firm fixed price (FFP)
Cost plus fixed fee (CPFF)

text: One of the important activities to be done in project closing is archiving project documents. Why should the project documents be archived?
explanation: The main goal of archiving project documents is to be used in future similar projects. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 149 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: To obtain the final acceptance from the customer.
To be used in future similar projects by the organization.
To update the organizational process assets.
To deliver to project sponsor.

text: Which of the following events is most related to agile principle "At regular intervals, the team reflects on how to become more effective, then tunes and adjusts its behavior accordingly"?
explanation: Agile methods employ “retrospectives” which are frequent lookbacks to reflect on how things are working on the project and identify opportunities for improvements. These retrospectives are typically done at the end of each iteration, ensuring that the team has regular opportunities to review their process. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 50

choices.text: Retrospective
Sprint review
Sprint planning meeting
Backlog refinement

text: During a retrospective, a stakeholder expressed his concerns about the quality of the product increments delivered so far. The stakeholder has mentioned that the team is not able to deliver high-quality product increment. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: The best answer is that the project leader and the team need to further discuss the quality related topics first and review the quality processes. If further assistance is needed they can involve the functional manager. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 299 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 87

choices.text: Involve the quality manager to investigate the project quality processes.
Arrange training sessions for the team members to improve the quality of the product increments.
Assign more quality subject matter experts to the project to improve quality processes.
The project leader should discuss this topic with the team and review the quality processes.

text: Robert is managing a large manufacturing project. Dave is the project sponsor and at the same time he is the direct manager of Robert. Dave wants to know about the earned value calculations. Robert provided him with this data the project total budget is 120,000$, the total amount spent till now is 70,000$ and the team has actually completed 50% worth of work. If the CPI is expected to be the same for the remainder of the project, what should the Estimate at Completion (EAC) be?
explanation: If the CPI is expected to be the same for the remainder of the project, so EAC = BAC/CPI. EV = 0.5 * 120,000, EV = 60,000$. BAC = 120,000$ and AC = 70,000$. CPI = EV/AC = 60,000/70,000 = 0.857. EAC = 120,000/0.857 = 140,000$ Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 265 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

text: When you add more risks to the risk register, all of the following will happen except:
explanation: As more risks are identified, the more you need for contingency reserve, the less you need to develop workarounds, your project will proceed with few surprises and you will need to develop more risk responses. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: More risk responses will need to be developed.
The need for contingency reserves is minimized.
The need for workarounds is minimized.
Your project will progress with few surprises.

text: You have just been authorized to manage a new software project for your company. Which of the following statements best describes your first action?
explanation: As you have just been authorized, so the project charter is issued, the project charter gives the project manager authority. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 563 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: Create the project scope statement.
Develop the project management plan.
Begin creating the project charter.
Figure out who has a stake in the project.

text: How should you plan for change management in a predictive environment?
explanation: Change is inevitable, so planning for change must be through a change management plan or a group of management plans where change management for each knowledge area can be performed separately. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 88.

choices.text: Changes are generally not predictable, therefore planning for change management cannot be reasonable.
Planning for change management should be done while the various change control processes are being applied.
Change management can be planned in a set of management plans or a specific change management plan.
Changes are a sign of poor planning. One should avoid changes during a project, thus eliminating the need to manage them.

text: The first value of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Agile teams value individuals and interactions over processes and tools". Which of the following agile techniques is closely related to this Agile Manifesto value?
explanation: While processes and tools will likely be necessary on our projects, we should try to focus the team's attention on the individuals and interactions involved. This is because projects are undertaken by people, not tools, and problems get solved by people, not processes. Likewise, projects are accepted by people, scope is debated by people, and accepted by people. So it is vital to agile projects success to have empowered teams. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 97

choices.text: Information radiators
Team empowerment
Timeboxing
Delivery cadence

text: Michael is managing an accounting software project. He finds that bringing all of his team members together into a single room to work increases their communication, and decreases misunderstandings. This is referred to as a:
explanation: Colocation involves placing many or all of the most active project team members in the same physical location to enhance their ability to perform as a team. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 340

choices.text: Colocation
Virtual team
Team building activities
Conflict resolution techniques

text: Dennis is managing a large and complex product development project. The project is very risky, however he could use the best practices in risk identification and has succesfully identified most of the project risks with the project team. The project has overall intensive planning. However, during project executing, the project has experienced several risks, which were already identified. Unfortunately, the project failed to achieve its objectives due to these realized risks. What has gone wrong?
explanation: As the project had intensive planning, this means that risks were properly defined, analyzed and responses were planned. The team spend effort in identifying and analyzing risks and developing risk responses, but no action is taken to manage the risk.

choices.text: The qualitative and quantitative risk analysis were inadequate.
There was not enough management reserve to accommodate the unknown risks.
No action has been taken to manage the identified risks.
There was not enough contingency reserve to accommodate the identified risks.

text: Why does Agile Manifesto value individuals and interactions over processes and tools?
explanation: While processes and tools will likely be necessary on our projects, we should try to focus the team’s attention on the individuals and interactions involved. This is because projects are undertaken by people, not tools, and problems get solved by people, not processes. Likewise, projects are accepted by people, scope is debated by people, and the definition of a successfully “done” project is negotiated by people. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Because agile projects don't need intense planning, so processes and tools don't have great importance.
Because normally processes and tools are less important for agile projects than people interactions.
Because processes and tools cannot help in successfully completing a project.
Because projects are undertaken by people, accepted by people, scope is debated by people, and the definition of “done” is negotiated by people.

text: You are an agile practitioner assigned to a software project to make some modifications to an online shopping company's website to increase revenue and enhance the customer experience, based on customer feedback. The modifications will be delivered over multiple releases. How can you measure the business value delivered during the project?
explanation: Agile approach follows the principle of incremental delivery which means that each release will deliver value. To measure the delivered value there should be a defined target to compare against. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

choices.text: After each release, review the achieved revenue and customer satisfaction ratings, comparing them to predefined targets.
At the end of the project, compare project revenues before and after the project.
Calculate the average revenue per release, and check whether the revenue of each release is above or below this average.
After each release, conduct a customer satisfaction survey and track the results over time.

text: Chris is managing a product development project. He receives a call from a team member informing him that they have found the same defect which has been identified before during a previous inspection. Chris is confused how this could have happened. He has raised a change request before to repair this defect and the change was approved. After investigation, he learns that the team did not implement the approved change. This situation indicates a gap in which process?
explanation: A part of the Control Quality process is to review approved changes. Approved change requests may include modifications such as defect repairs, revised work methods, and revised schedules. The implementation of approved changes should be verified, confirmed for completeness, retested, and certified as correct. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 301

choices.text: Plan Quality Management
Control Scope
Control Quality
Perform Integrated Change Control

text: Which of the following best describes an opportunity on a project?
explanation: The quality requirements are hard to achieve, the requirements might need updating later, and an increase in USD consumer price all have negative impacts on the project. They are all threats not opportunities. While a team member will learn more about something, it will be useful in future projects, so it is considered an opportunity. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 397 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: The quality requirements are hard to achieve.
The requirements might need updating later.
An increase in USD consumer price may occur, which will need $5,000 contingency reserve.
A team member will learn more about microwaves and can use this information on future projects.

text: Which of the following statements is true regarding project communications in projects with adaptive life cycle?
explanation: Project environments subject to various elements of ambiguity and change have an inherent need to communicate evolving and emerging details more frequently and quickly. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 365

choices.text: Projects with predictive life cycle have an inherent need to communicate evolving and emerging details more frequently and quickly than projects with iterative or incremental life cycle.
Projects with predictive life cycle have an inherent need to communicate evolving and emerging details more frequently and quickly than projects with adaptive life cycle.
There is no difference between communication in projects with adaptive life cycle and communication in projects with predictive life cycle.
Projects with adaptive life cycle have an inherent need to communicate evolving and emerging details more frequently and quickly than projects with predictive life cycle.

text: You have just been promoted to a project manager position. You are assigned to manage your first project. You want to report your project schedule. You asked a more experienced project manager about the best method to report your schedule. He told you there are several methods to report your schedule like network diagrams, milestone chart and Gantt chart. Under which circumstances is it best to use a network diagram rather than a Gantt chart?
explanation: In this question you can read only the last sentence without reading the rest of question. A network diagram is used to show interdependencies between activities. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 218 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Gantt chart is always the best way to report project schedule.
To show interdependencies between activities.
To track progress or to report to the team.
To report to the sponsor.

text: The first principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Our highest priority is to satisfy the customer through early and continuous delivery of valuable software". Which of the following agile practices and techniques is closely related to this principle?
explanation: This principle includes three main points: The first is to satisfy the customer. The second point is early and continuous delivery. The final point is that what agile teams are delivering is valuable software (deliverable). Planning the project into releases and iterations with the highest value features to be delivered first allows early and continuous delivery. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55 and 80

choices.text: Burndown chart
Information radiators
Daily standup
Iteration planning

text: Paul is managing an industrial project. In the middle of the project, a project team member noticed that there is a sudden increase in the usage of fuel in a machine. The machine normally consumes 3 litres per day, and its consumption became 5 litres per day with the same level of effort. The team member informed Paul about the problem. In order to control the usage of fuel, Paul asked the team member to cooperate with the technical support team to investigate the problem. They found a defect in a component in the machine which caused the abnormal usage when it is fixed the machine worked normally with the original consumption rate. Which process are they involved in?
explanation: Control Resources is concerned with influencing the factors that can create resources utilization change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 354

choices.text: Control Resources
Control Quality
Manage Quality
Control Scope

text: You are managing a new construction project that is budgeted at $3,000,000. Too many activities are being executed underway on your project. You are getting worried about the accuracy of the progress reporting your team members are doing. How could you verify whether there is a problem or not?
explanation: Perform a quality audit as part of the Manage Quality process to verify that there is a problem or not. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 294 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 87

choices.text: Feasibility studies
Quality audits
Sensitivity analysis
Monte Carlo Analysis

text: You are managing a four phases construction project to build a new industrial park. The project is very large and involves more than four hundreds stakeholders and four subcontractors. A team member who has never worked before in large projects like this one ask you about when the project kick-off meeting will be held. What would be your answer?
explanation: Multi-phase projects will include a kick-off meeting at the beginning of each phase. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 86 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page

choices.text: Before project initiating.
At the end of the project.
At the beginning of each phase.
At the end of project planning and start of project executing.

text: What is meant by cost risk?
explanation: The first thing to think of is: cost risks are risks or costs? Of course they are risks, so all answers saying it is cost should be eliminated. It is true that some risks cost the project money but this is not the right definition of cost risks. The right definition is that there is a risk that project costs could go higher than planned. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition

choices.text: The cost of all the identified risks if they happen.
There is a risk that project costs could go higher than planned.
There are risks that will cost the project money.
The cost of contingency reserve assigned for residual risks.

text: The product owner wants to know when a user story can be completed. The development team informed him that this user story has been assigned 5 story points. The product owner asks the team if there is any time estimate or there is any relation between the sizing and the time required to complete the user story. Which of the following statements represents the team’s best answer?
explanation: To estimate more efficiently and accurately, agile teams rely on relative sizing. They do most of their estimating not in hours or days, but in a relative unit called “story points.” Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: There is no direct relation between sizing and time. You can refer to the team velocity, it will specify how many story points can be completed within an iteration.
We can give this user story high priority to be completed as soon as possible.
A story point can be completed by 1 person within 1 business day.
A story point can be completed by 1 person within 1 business hour.

text: Which of the following is a risk response strategy that can be used for both opportunities and threats?
explanation: Only Acceptance and Escalation are valid risk response strategy for both opportunities and threats. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 442-444 and PMBOK 7th, Page 123-125

choices.text: Mitigation and Transference
Transference and Escalation
Sharing and Acceptance
Acceptance and Escalation

text: An organization has signed a contract with the customer to develop a new mobile application. The customer has stated in the contract that there are milestones to be approved by the customer like creating the project charter, approving the project management plan, approving the demo version,..etc. The project team assigned from the performing organization need to use iterative approach because it is more tailored to the project needs, which may lead to missing some of these milestones. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Additional constraints for tailoring for the organization include projects performed under contract. Projects that are performed under contract may have contract terms that specify the use of a particular life cycle, delivery approach, or methodology. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 139

choices.text: Follow the contract.
Use hybrid approach as a compromise.
Use iterative approach.
Reject the project assignment.

text: A project is on its third iteration review. One of the stakeholders has raised a concern about the product increment of this iteration. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: It is needed to know the stakeholder's concern in more details. So, arranging a meeting with the stakeholder and the product owner to know his concern in details and then the product owner can add the stakeholder's requirement in the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Add an entry in the issue log and reassess requirements collection process.
Hold a meeting with the team and the product owner to address the stakeholder concern.
Ask the product owner to add the stakeholder concern to the next iteration.
The agile coach should facilitate a meeting with the stakeholder and the product owner to discuss his concern.

text: During a sprint retrospective, the project team discuss the issues they faced during the sprint. One of these issues is that the team has committed to complete 50 story points, however they completed only 30 story points. What might be the most likely root cause of this issue?
explanation: As the team has committed to complete a certain number of story points in a sprint, so the team members know their capacity and capabilities. So, there should not be any need for additional resources. Agile teams normally estimate their tasks in real time, so meetings should not have an effect on their estimates. The best answer is that the team might have underestimated their work. It was more complex than what they estimated, that is why it was not completed during the sprint. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: The complexity of the work was underestimated.
There were many meetings and interruptions during the sprint.
More resources were needed to complete the committed work.
Some team members were assigned later after the sprint has started.

text: During daily standup, a project team member A has mentioned an impediment that the product owner and a lead developer will leave the organization this month. Team member B has mentioned an impediment of delay in required components which will affect product increment delivery. Team member C has mentioned an impediment that the logistics provider contract will expire this week. Team member D has mentioned an impediment that the number of defects found till now is higher than expected. Which impediment should the scrum master focus on first?
explanation: Finding a product owner is the most important impediment for the scrum master to work on. The product owner is responsible for prioritizing the product backlog items and helps the teams see how to deliver the highest value without creating waste. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide, Page 41

choices.text: Finding a product owner.
Asking procurement department to renew the contract with the logistics supplier.
Finding the root cause of the defects.
Finding a replacement for the delayed component.

text: Stakeholders are any people or organizations whose interests may be positively or negatively impacted by the project or product. When do they have the most influence on a project?
explanation: Stakeholders have influence throughout the project, but they must be identified and involved at the beginning of the project to determine their requirements and expectations at the beginning. If this effort was not done, you will discover their requirements later which will be more costly than if they are included at the beginning of the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 549

choices.text: Throughout the project
During the executing process group
At the beginning of the project
During the monitoring and controlling process group

text: At the beginning of the project a team member asks you, the project manager, when we should identify risks. Which of the following statements best describes your answer?
explanation: When there is any change to any project constraint, you must evaluate the impacts of this change to all project constraints. This means you must know all risks associated with the change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: Whenever there is a change to the project.
After the project charter is received and before the WBS is created.
Before the project charter is created.
During the Validate Scope process.

text: Sam is a functional manager in the organization, he called Henry, the project manager three times today requiring to release one of his resources from his project to assign some functional work to him. He called Henry ten times this week for the same purpose. A team member informed Henry that the hardware needed to perform an activity may be delayed. This activity has early start of 5 and late start of 35. The project float is zero. The project is going well and its risk rating is low. The project has SPI of 0.89 and CPI of 1.12. What is the best thing to do?
explanation: It is necessary to identify the most critical problem that needs to be addressed. Is it the impact of phone calls? Of course not, as the project manager spends 90% of their time communicating with others, so meeting with Sam to address the effects of phone calls is not the best solution. Activity crashing that requires the equipment is an incorrect answer because this activity has different early start and late start dates, meaning it has float and is therefore not on the critical path. Looking for opportunities to reduce costs is also not the best choice because the Cost Performance Index (CPI) is greater than 1, indicating the project is under budget. The best choice here is project schedule compression because the Schedule Performance Index (SPI) is less than 1.

choices.text: Crash the activity which needs the hardware.
Henry should meet Sam to solve the problem.
Look for areas to cut costs.
Crash the project.

text: Midway through the project, a conflict arises between the project manager and a team member. The project manager believes that a specific task should be executed by the team member, while the team member is convinced that it should be executed by another person. The team member escalates the situation to his functional manager. In order to resolve the conflict, the project manager should consult _________.
explanation: A RACI chart is a useful tool to use to ensure clear assignment of roles and responsibilities when the team consists of internal and external resources. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 317 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

choices.text: Resource calendars
Resource management plan
Resource breakdown structure
RACI chart

text: A project manager for a product development project was informed by a team member that a defect was found in a deliverable that must be delivered to customer tomorrow according to the project schedule. The project manager knows that the customer doesn’t have the technical understanding to notice the defect. The deliverable meets the contract requirements, but it doesn’t meet the project manager’s quality standard. What is the best course of action for the project manager?
explanation: Issuing the deliverable although it doesn't meet quality requirements is against the professional and social responsibility of a project manager. Documenting the problem in lessons learned register is a good solution for future projects, but it doesn't solve the current situation. Informing the customer that the deliverable will be late may breach the contract and harm the relationship, so the best option is to discuss the issue with the customer. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 299

choices.text: Issue the deliverable and get formal acceptance from the customer, as the deliverable meets his quality standard.
Inform the customer that the deliverable will be late and fix the fault.
Discuss the issue with the customer.
Note the problem in the lessons learned register so future projects do not face the same problem.

text: There are over 40 stakeholders on a new Java training project, and no one can agree on project objectives. One stakeholder believes the project can achieve a 30% improvement in team’s knowledge of Java while another believes a 40% improvement is possible. The project manager believes a 20% improvement is more realistic. What is the best course of action?
explanation: The best action is to perform a feasibility study. Other choices are all subjective evaluation. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 30 and The Standard for Project Management, Page 34

choices.text: Perform feasibility analysis
Use the average of these numbers
Ask the sponsor to make the final decision
Use your decision as you are the project manager

text: You are managing an industrial project. You performed till now work valued at $15,000, which was planned to cost $12,000. You actually paid $11,000. What is the status of this project?
explanation: SPI = EV / PV = $15,000 / $12,000 = 1.25—so your project is ahead of schedule. CPI = EV / AC = $15,000 / $11,000 = 1.36—so your project is within its budget. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: The project is ahead of schedule and within its budget.
The project is behind schedule and within its budget.
The project is ahead of schedule and over its budget.
The project is behind schedule and over its budget.

text: Which of the following events is least likely to be used as a source for identifying threats?
explanation: Daily standup meetings can be used in any project and are a source for identifying potential threats and opportunities. Frequent demonstrations of increments of the product or service, interim designs, or proof of concepts can surface threats and opportunities. Retrospectives and lessons learned meetings can be used to identify threats to performance, project team cohesion, etc., and to seek improvements. Product backlog refinement is mainly used to prioritize items in product backlog. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 127

choices.text: Daily Standups
Demonstrations
Retrospectives
Backlog Refinement

text: Matt is a project manager for a large tele-communication project. He and his team are now determining the types, quantities and skills of the needed resources for each project activity. They will need to store their spare parts in a warehouse as it will be needed for all the project duration. They are now evaluating whether to buy a warehouse and hire employees to manage it or to outsource the warehousing activities to another company. Which process are they involved in and which tool are they using?
explanation: As they are determining the types, quantities and skills of the needed resources, so they are involved in the Estimate Activity Resources. As they are evaluating identified options in order to select the options or approaches to use to execute and perform the work of the project, so they are using alternatives analysis which is an example of data analysis. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition Page 325

choices.text: Estimate Activity Resources and Data Analysis
Plan Resources Management and Data Representation
Acquire Resources and Decision Making
Estimate Activity Resources and Analogous Estimating

text: During the Identify Risks process, a project manager and stakeholders used many methods to identify risks and they identified more than 1000 risks. Then they made sure that all the risks were understood and they identified triggers. Later, in the Plan Risk Responses process, the project manager avoided some identified risks and mitigated others. What has he done wrong?
explanation: More people should be involved in the Plan Risk Responses process, those people may have more experience in dealing with risks. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 441

choices.text: The project manager should have involved more people in the Plan Risk Responses process.
He should identify triggers in the Plan Risk Responses process not in the Identify Risks process.
The project manager should have performed a risk audit.
The project manager should have created workarounds.

text: Steven is a project manager for a large scale integration project, which has a tight schedule. He is reporting to Derek, a senior manager who is also the sponsor of the project. Derek called Steven in the middle of the project and asked him about the status of the project, as he doesn't know whether the project is ahead or behind schedule at this moment. Derek wants to know the earned value numbers. Which report should Steven send to him?
explanation: S-Curve is used to display EV, PV and AC data of a certain project. Other mentioned types of reports don't contain earned value numbers. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 263 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

choices.text: Project Progress Report
S-Curve
Project Status Report
Milestone Chart

text: You are a project manager of a high-risk electronics project which has a lot of new technologies. You created a risk management plan, identified 225 risks, and documented them in a risk register and documented sources of overall project risk, together with summary information on identified individual project risks in a risk report. Then, the risks were analyzed and responses were planned. During risk control meetings. It gets obvious to everyone that the documents you created are not helpful. What have you probably done wrong?
explanation: You should involve everyone in identifying risks. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411

choices.text: You didn't perform quantitative risk analysis for the identified risks.
You did the first processes alone.
You did not identify Triggers.
Risk response owners didn't implement the planned responses.

text: All of the following statements are true about procurement inspection, except:
explanation: An inspection is a structured review of the work being performed by the contractor. This may involve a simple review of the deliverables or an actual physical review of the work itself. On a construction/engineering/infrastructure project, inspections involve walkthroughs of the site by both the buyer and the contractor to ensure a mutual understanding of the work in progress. An audit is a structured review of the procurement process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498

choices.text: An inspection is a structured review of the work being performed by the contractor.
An inspection may involve a walkthroughs of the site by both the buyer and the contractor.
An inspection may involve a simple review of the deliverables or an actual physical review of the work itself.
An inspection is a structured review of the procurement process.

text: Lisa is managing a construction project. The project is to build a new office building and it is very important to the customer. The project team has just calculated the critical path duration and informed the customer that the project will take one year. The customer informed Lisa that the project duration is too long and the building should be opened with an official ceremony after ten months. What should she do?
explanation: Adding resources to some critical path activities will shorten the project schedule, this practice is known as crashing. Fast tracking the project can't be done if the project risk is high, because fast tracking increases project risk. Escalating the issue to the project sponsor is not a proactive solution. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Fast track the critical path activities if overall project risk is high.
Add resources to some critical path activities to adjust the project end date to meet the imposed date by the customer.
Inform the customer that the critical path is the minimum project duration and it has no flexibility.
Escalate the issue and ask for guidance from the project sponsor.

text: After the newly formed project team has gone through many conflicts between the team members, the project team starts to function as a collective body. Team members know their places on the team and how they relate to and interface with all the other members. They are starting to work together. What is the best leadership style to lead a team at this stage?
explanation: When the team reaches the norming phase, the members have successfully created rules (team norms) to help govern themselves. This doesn’t mean the team leader can simply go into cruise control mode, however. Instead, the leader needs to play a supporting role. The team will still need help with conflict resolution, as well as reminders to enforce the norms they have created. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 166

choices.text: Provide a more directive than supportive leadership.
Provide a more supportive than directive leadership.
Provide high levels of both directive and supportive leadership.
Provide low levels of both directive and supportive leadership.

text: You have been assigned as a project manager for a new network roll-out project. You are in the process of creating the project charter. You meet with the key stakeholders to solicit their inputs. You meet with the IT manager to know the cost of the IT activities in your project. He informs you that it is impossible to provide a cost estimate without knowing the specific resources who will be assigned to the project. You explain that:
explanation: In project charter you only need ROM estimate. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 241 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 46

choices.text: For now only you need a ROM estimate to be validated later, when you know what resources will be available.
Try first to estimate the needed resources for the IT manager to get accurate estimates.
It is not a problem because stakeholder identification during project initiating will uncover key resources.
Resource cost is not an issue at this point. You will have a realistic budget at the end of project planning.

text: Laurence has been assigned to manage his first project using a waterfall approach. The project charter has been issued. Laurence and his team are identifying the project stakeholders. A team member asks Laurence why he is checking the agreements documents while they are identifying project stakeholders. Which of the following represents the best answer to the team member's question?
explanation: The parties of an agreement are project stakeholders. The agreement can contain references to additional stakeholders. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 510 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: The agreement includes a complete list of project stakeholders.
The agreement includes the results of market research to identify additional stakeholders.
Because some of project stakeholders may be involved in the agreement.
Because all parties of the agreement are stakeholders.

text: You are a project manager for a product development project. You have a tight budget and the project is behind schedule. One of your team members presents a new software that will help automate some of the project work. By using the new software, the project work will be done faster than planned. The new software will cost 10,000$ and it will help you save 45,000$ in total costs, it will also save 45 days from the total project duration. You decide to purchase the new software and change the project management plan accordingly after the approval of CCB. Which risk response strategy is used in this situation?
explanation: In this situation the project manager changed the project management plan to make sure that the opportunity happens. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 444 and PMBOK 7th, Page 125

choices.text: Enhancing
Exploiting
Acceptance
Sharing

text: You are a project manager working on a project to move a call center to a new city. You include a representative from a transportation company as a stakeholder. The transportation company will transfer computers and furniture from the old place to the new one. You include meetings with that representative in the WBS. The sponsor objects to such activities as unnecessary. The best response is to inform the sponsor that:
explanation: The transportation company representative is a stakeholder because he can negatively impact the project. So you need to communicate more with him to increase his engagement. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 8
