choices.text: The transportation company representative is a stakeholder since the call center will be in his city.
The transportation company representative is a stakeholder because he will be a customer for the call center.
The transportation company representative knows the call center needs better than either of us.
The transportation company representative can negatively impact the project.

text: A new project has been initiated in XYZ organization. The organization has its internal governance system which states that each project must pass through five approval gates from initiation to closure. In order to have gate 2 approved, the project team must provide a project management plan including scope, schedule and cost baselines. The project team objects to this approach and decides that they will use adaptive approach which is more suitable and tailored to the project needs. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Organizations that have established process governance need to ensure tailoring is aligned to policy. To demonstrate that the project team’s tailoring decisions do not threaten the organization’s larger strategic or stewardship goals, project teams may need to justify using a tailored approach. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 139

choices.text: The organization should follow the project team's tailored approach.
The project team should follow the organization's governance system.
The organization should exclude this project from internal governance system.
Let the customer decide which development approach to adopt.

text: Gary is a project manager for a large mechanical project. He is documenting the roles, responsibilities, required skills and reporting relationships for the needed resources. Where should he document this information?
explanation: Roles, responsibilities, required skills and reporting relationships for the needed resources are documented in the resources management plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 318 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Resource Requirements
RACI chart
Responsibility Assignment Matrix
Resources Management Plan

text: Carlos is the project manager of a system automation project that was scheduled to be completed within six months. During the early stages of the project, he and the stakeholders operated with the belief that the software that the project team is developing will be compatible with the existing hardware. Now after the project team has started developing the software it has become apparent that the software will not work with part of the existing hardware. The incorrect belief of Carlos and the stakeholders about software compatibility is considered as a/an:
explanation: An assumption is a factor in the planning process that is considered to be true, real, or certain, without proof or demonstration. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 415 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 174

choices.text: Assumption
Issue
Risk
Constraint

text: Edwin is managing a large construction project. He and his team are in the planning phase. He is estimating the cost of each activity with the project team. The project sponsor has requested from him to take uncertainty into consideration. Which of the following techniques is the best to use?
explanation: The accuracy of single-point cost estimates may be improved by considering estimation uncertainty and risk and using three estimates to define an approximate range for an activity’s cost. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 244

choices.text: Parametric Estimating
Analogous Estimating
Triangular distribution
Bottom-up Estimating

text: The project manager assigned Mark to send a weekly email update to the project key stakeholders to explain the status of the project. Which communication method does this email update represent?
explanation: Push communication is sent or distributed directly to specific recipients who need to receive the information. This ensures that the information is distributed but does not ensure that it actually reached or was understood by the intended audience. Push communications artifacts include letters, memos, reports, emails, faxes, voice mails, blogs, and press releases. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: Push communication
Pull communication
Formal communication
Interactive communication

text: You are a project manager for a new software project to develop a website for a customer. The customer wants the project to be completed in 40 days. The project is going well and the cost performance index CPI = 1.07 and the project is being performed with four team members, the project critical path duration is 38 days with a standard deviation of two days. What is the maximum project float?
explanation: Mean= 38, the project will take 38+/- 2, range 36 to 40. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 210 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Four days
Zero days
Two days
One day

text: After the prospective sellers have submitted proposals, the engineering manager is arguing with the finance manager about which seller should award the contract. The engineering manager wants the contract to be awarded to seller A because this seller has great experience with the work to be performed. While the finance manager says that the contract should be awarded to seller B, because he will provide the required work with low cost. The project manager should tell them to best focus on which document to make the selection:
explanation: Source selection criteria is the document which determines how to select a seller. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 478 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 75

choices.text: Procurement strategy
Source selection criteria
Procurement statement of work
Procurement management plan

text: During measuring the project performance against the project management plan, you discover that the project is running behind schedule and the final deliverable will be delayed by one month after the required date by the customer. What should the project manager do?
explanation: Working overtime or requesting to change the project end dates are reactive solutions, even evaluating the impacts of completing the project later is not a good solution. The project manager must be proactive, so evaluating the impacts for crashing or fast tracking is the best solution. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 223 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Look for options with project team members like adding more resources or performing critical path activities in parallel.
Let the team members work overtime to finish the project on time.
Evaluate the impact of completing the project later than the required date.
Make a change request to extend the project and schedule and submit it to the change control board.

text: Prior to the product release, the project team has requested detailed feedback from all key stakeholders. All the key stakeholders have provided their feedback except one key stakeholders. The project team members have reminded him many times to provide feedback, but he didn't. Which of the following is the best course of action for the project manager?
explanation: The best solution is to resolve the conflict in a direct collaborative approach. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 348 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

choices.text: Exclude this particular stakeholder's feedback and proceed to product release.
Escalate this topic to the project sponsor.
Hold a meeting with the project team and the stakeholder to resolve the conflict.
Call the stakeholder privately to understand the reason of the conflict.

text: All of the following are not “hygiene factors” under Herzberg’s Motivation-Hygiene Theory except:
explanation: Motivational factors include matters that relate to the content of the work, such as achievement, growth, and advancement. Herzberg also identified hygiene factors related to the work, such as company policies, salary, and the physical environment. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 158

choices.text: Recognition for excellent work
Self-actualization
Company policy
Achievements

text: Communication barriers can cause miscommunications, it may influence communication when sending or receiving information. Which of the following is not a barrier?
explanation: Feedback is a tool for improving communication not a barrier. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 373

choices.text: Prejudices and Hostility
Distance
Culture and Language
Feedback

text: During developing the communication management plan for your software project, a team member asks you whether social media can be included as a communication tool to the project communication strategy. What would be your answer?
explanation: The standard communication strategy for project stakeholder communications embraces and selects from all technologies and respects cultural, practical, and personal preferences for language, media, content, and delivery. When appropriate, social media and other advanced computing technologies may be included. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 364

choices.text: Yes, social media can be included in the communication strategy because everyone is using social media in communication.
Yes, social media can be included in the communication strategy with respect to cultural, practical, and personal preferences for language, media, content, and delivery.
No, social media can't be included in the communication strategy because it is informal communication. You must select a formal communication tool.
No, social media can't be included in the communication strategy because it is not available in some countries.

text: You are a project manager for a tele-communication project. Your project is to extend cables for a landline phone network. The total cost of the project is $52/ meter to lay 4 km of cable under ground. It’s scheduled to take 8 weeks to complete, with an equal amount of cable laid in each week. It’s currently week 5, and your team has laid 1,800 meters of cable so far. What is the SPI of your project?
explanation: EV = 1800 * 52 = 93600 PV = 5/8 * 52 * 4000 = 130000 SPI = EV/PV = 93600/130000 = 0.72 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

text: Why will you need to update the WBS after you complete the risk response planning with your team?
explanation: Plan Risk Responses process may result in updates to the project management plan components like WBS because of new, modified or omitted work generated by the risk responses, the scope baseline may be updated to reflect those changes. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 447

choices.text: Because of work that was removed during the WBS creation.
Because of new work added by the risk responses.
Because of risks associated with work packages.
Because of risks generated from decomposing work packages to activities.

text: An organization is going to undertake a project for regulatory compliance using agile approach which doesn’t have an easily determined monetary value. What can the organization do to assess the value of the project?
explanation: Some projects are undertaken for safety or regulatory compliance purposes and don’t have an easily determined monetary value. To assess the value of these projects, the organization might look at the financial ramifications of not undertaking the project. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

choices.text: Use traditional approach.
Evaluate financial impacts of not undertaking the project.
Find a way to calculate Return on Investment (ROI).
Don’t implement the project as it doesn’t deliver business value.

text: The project team has found that the team velocity has dropped significantly and they have a critical deliverable due in two weeks. The team members decide to skip final test in order to bring the project back on track and don't miss the deliverable due date. Which of the following is a likely result of the team decision?
explanation: Removing a test will lead to technical debt which means degraded deliverables quality. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 58

choices.text: Increased technical debt
Increased customer satisfaction
Increased return on investment
Increased resources cost

text: What does the Agile Manifesto value "responding to change over following a plan" mean?
explanation: Instead of investing effort in trying to bring the project back in line with the original plan, we want to spend more of our effort and energy responding to the changes that will arise. It doesn’t mean we should not do any planning and just react to changes. We still need to plan, but we also need to acknowledge that our initial plans were made when we knew least about the project (at the beginning) and will need to be updated as the work progresses. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 20.

choices.text: Agile projects don't need intense planning, so change requests are more important as they represent customer inputs.
The project team commits to the initially developed plan, however any change request to the project plan can be approved or rejected.
In agile projects, initial planning is done when we know the least about the project and this plan may be updated as the work progresses.
In agile projects, the project team spends less time in planning and approves more change requests than traditional projects.

text: During product demonstration, the team presents the product increment to the stakeholders. The customer requests some new features to be added. Which of the following is the best course of action? (1) The team should analyze the impact of adding the new features. (2) Add the new features to the next iteration. (3) Submit a change request. (4) Ask the customer about the priority of the new features. (5) The product owner should maintain the product backlog.
explanation: As the team completes the features usually in the form of user stories, the team periodically demonstrates the working product. The product owner sees the demonstration and accepts or declines stories. If the stakeholders want to add new features, they should clarify their priorities and accordingly the product owner will add them to the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55.

choices.text: All of them.

text: The project team is undergoing an agile transformation. This requires the team members to maintain their original project plans and project documents in the current management tool, but also enter performance data in the new tool built around agile practices. Which of the following documents the project team members will not map into the new agile tool?
explanation: Requirements documentation will be added and maintained by the product owner to the product backlog. It is the product owner's responsibility not the team. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 52

choices.text: Project charter
Team charter
Risk register
Requirements documentation

text: An activity has an early start (ES) of day 2, and a late finish (LF) of day 18. The activity duration is 5 days. What is the activity’s float?
explanation: Activity duration = Early finish – Early start = Late finish – Late start. Activity float = Late finish – Early finish = Late start – Early start. Activity Float = LF – EF = LS – ES. Activity Duration = LF – LS = EF – ES. 5 = EF – 2. EF = 2 + 5 = 7. Activity Float = LF – EF = 18 – 7 = 11. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 210

text: During project executing, a project manager realizes that he needs to decrease project costs. After analyzing all options, he decides to select one of the following options to decrease project costs. Which one of the following will decrease project costs?
explanation: Choosing the component A will not reduce the project cost. Choosing a more experienced resource of course will increase the cost. Performing activities on parallel will increase risks that will increase the costs. So the best choice is to remove a test from the project management plan although it seems not to be the best solution, this will affect quality negatively but will reduce cost. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 241

choices.text: Remove a test from the project management plan.
Change to component A from component B. Component A costs more to purchase, but has a lower life cycle cost than B.
Change activity A to be completed by resource B instead of resource C. Resource B is a more experienced worker.
Move activities B and H to occur concurrently, and accept a 30% increase in the risk that 5 more resources will be needed later.

text: Many project team members requested from the project manager to send them the training material.The project manager has uploaded all the training material on the company's intranet to be accessible to all team members. This is an example of:
explanation: Pull communication. Used for large complex information sets, or for large audiences, and requires the recipients to access content at their own discretion subject to security procedures. These methods include web portals, intranet sites, e-learning, lessons learned databases, or knowledge repositories. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: Interactive communications
Push communications
Pull communications
Formal communications

text: A scrum team is working on a large project which requires 5 scrum sub-teams working in parallel. How many product backlogs should they work on?
explanation: If scrum teams become too large, they should consider reorganizing into multiple cohesive scrum teams, each focused on the same product. Therefore, they should share the same product goal, product backlog, and product owner. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 113

choices.text: Either 1 or 5 according to the product owner preference.
Either 1 or 5 according to the scrum master preference.

text: Which of the following is an example of a reward system?
explanation: Reward must be achievable and motivating. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 341 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 158

choices.text: The team member who works hardest will receive $1,000.
Everyone will get a bonus of $300 if the project finishes in on time.
All the team members will have a salary raise if the company achieved good revenue.
All the project team members will get 500$ if the product of the project was state-of-the-art.

text: Two team members of your project team are discussing together about the correct order of the Monitoring and Controlling processes for Scope Management. One of them said that the Validate Scope process should be performed first, while the other member said that the Control Scope process should be performed first. Which of the following is the correct order of Monitoring and Controlling processes for Scope Management?
explanation: Validate Scope occurs with each deliverable or phase review and Control Scope is an ongoing process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 131

choices.text: First Validate Scope, then Control Scope.
First Control Scope, then Validate Scope.
Both happen simultaneously.
There is not enough information to decide.

text: The project team is discussing the goals for the next iteration with the customer and product owner, then they will select the user stories they will commit to deliver as part of the next iteration. This behavior does comply with which agile principle?
explanation: As the team is self organized, it would be important for the team to discuss the goals for the next iteration with the customer and then select the user stories they will commit to deliver as part of the next iteration. The team is self organized, the product owner can't just select the next top story points’ worth of functionality off the top of the backlog and then tell the team to get on with the work. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 54

choices.text: Our highest priority is to satisfy the customer through early and continuous delivery of valuable software.
Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer's competitive advantage.
Simplicity—the art of maximizing the amount of work not done—is essential.
The best architectures, requirements, and designs emerge from self-organizing teams.

text: During a retrospective, the project team discusses the high rate of scrap during the last iteration. This rate is the highest all over the project. Which of the following should the project team use to tackle this issue?
explanation: The project team may use techniques like value stream mapping to visualize the work, identify the issues, identify the wastes, and propose solutions. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 151

choices.text: Story map
Burndown charts
Value stream mapping
Burnup charts

text: You are a project manager for a large construction project. You are searching for a supplier to provide some raw material for your project. You asked your team to prepare all the needed information on the types, quantities and specifications of the needed raw material. When will you send him a RFQ?
explanation: The procurement management process must be performed with the correct sequence. You must first plan for procurement management and perform make or buy analysis to know if you will buy or make what you need, then create procurement SOW and procurement documents and send them to all prospective sellers. Then you can select a seller. So you will send an RFQ to seller after creating the procurement documents but before selecting a seller. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 477 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 192

choices.text: After you create the procurement documents, but before you select the seller.
Before signing the contract, but after selecting the seller.
After the bidder conference, but before you select the seller.
After making make-or-buy analysis and before taking make-or-buy decision.

text: Your manager calls you into her office to informally discuss a new potential project for your organization. The project involves moving a call center to a new building 30 km away, installing new equipment and devices and implement a new monitoring software, and giving the staff five days of intensive, product specific training. She asks your opinion of this work. How should you respond?
explanation: Many projects mentioned here not only one project. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 4 and The Standard for Project Management, Page 4

choices.text: You should inform your manager that this initiative may involve more than one project.
You need to meet with the IT and call center managers to get more details on the plan.
You should suggest that the project needs a formal charter.
You should tell your manager that the staff intensive training should comply with the recognition and reward system.

text: A project has been initiated within an organization to merge two large departments into one department. There are many side talks between the employees in the two departments affected by the change. Most of them feel anxiety because there will be changes to their usual way of working, working on new systems which they are not familiar with and changing their work locations. Which of the following models can help them understand what they are feeling and enable them to move through change more efficiently?
explanation: Virginia Satir developed a model of how people experience and cope with change. Its purpose is to help project team members understand what they are feeling and enable them to move through change more efficiently. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 163

choices.text: OSCAR
Virginia Satir
Cynefin Framework
Herzberg

text: During executing a mechanical project using waterfall approach, an experienced engineer calls the project manager in private and informs him that there is a simpler design which is more fuel-efficient and it could achieve great cost saving. What should the project manager do?
explanation: It is not the responsibility of the engineer to evaluate impacts of the change on project constraints. A change request should be raised with the engineer suggestion for the new design and it will be assessed and approved or rejected by change control board. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66

choices.text: Do nothing, the plan has already been approved including the old design.
Ask the engineer to submit a change request.
Ask the engineer to evaluate the impacts on other project constraints.
Hold a meeting with the engineer and the designer of the old system to discover variances.

text: Which of the following statements is true?
explanation: A complex project may involve managing multiple contracts simultaneously or in sequence. In such cases, each contract life cycle may begin and end during any phase of the project life cycle. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 75

choices.text: A. A complex project may involve managing multiple contracts.
B. A complex contract may involve managing multiple projects.
C. A project can involve only one contract.
D. A contract can involve only one project.

text: Ivan is the project manager for a new project to develop electronic tickets instead of paper tickets. He identified all stakeholders of the project and is analyzing the current and desired level of engagement for each stakeholder. Piere is the leader of the printing team, he is aware of the project and thinks that he may be released from the organization after the project is completed becuase there will be no more need for paper tickets. Piere is trying to put obstacles in the way of project completion in order not to be fired from the organization. How should Ivan classify the engagement level of Piere?
explanation: Resistant stakeholder is aware of the project and potential impacts but resistant to any changes that may occur as a result of the work or outcomes of the project. These stakeholders will be unsupportive of the work or outcomes of the project. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 521 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11–12

choices.text: Unaware
Resistant
Neutral
Supportive

text: The lowest level of the risk breakdown structure can be used as a/an:
explanation: The risk categories in the lowest level of the risk breakdown structure can be used as a prompt list for individual project risks. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 416

choices.text: Prioritized list of project risks
Individual project risks
Checklist
Prompt list

text: Bill is the project manager for a construction project that is building a new branch for his company. When the project first started, he met with the stakeholders to define the scope. The CEO of the company will attend the ceremony to open the new branch. Bill was informed by his manager that he has a deadline to finish the new branch in ten months. This deadline is considered as a:
explanation: Deadline is a project constraint. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 28

choices.text: Project Schedule
Project Objective
Project Constraint
Project Assumption

text: What is the purpose of the questions asked during the daily standup?
explanation: During daily standup the scrum team discuss what did I complete since the last standup? What am I planning to complete between now and the next standup? And What are my impediments (or risks or problems)? An antipattern typically seen in standups is that the team begins to solve problems as they become apparent. Standups are for realizing there are problems—not for solving them. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 54

choices.text: Define impediments and discuss planned work
Identify problems and discuss accomplishments
Remove impediments and discuss achievements
Fix problems and discuss achievements

text: Which of the following statements best describes the difference between Validate Scope and Control Quality?
explanation: Validate Scope is concerned with the acceptance of deliverables while the Control Quality is concerned with correctness of the deliverables. Verified deliverables is an output of the Control Quality process and input to the Validate Scope process. Generally Control Quality is performed before the Validate Scope process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 164

choices.text: Validate Scope is generally performed before Control Quality.
Validate Scope is concerned with the acceptance of deliverables while the Control Quality is concerned with correctness of the deliverables.
Verified deliverables are outputs of the Validate Scope process and input to the Control Quality process.
Accepted deliverables are outputs of the Control Quality process and input to the Validate Scope process.

text: All of the following statements describe the focus of the contract review and approval process, except:
explanation: The primary focus of the review and approval process is to ensure that the contract adequately describes the products, services, or results that the seller is agreeing to provide, while being in compliance with the laws and regulations regarding procurements. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461

choices.text: To ensure that the contract adequately describes the products, services, or results that the seller will provide.
To ensure that the procurement office got the least possible price from the prospective sellers.
To ensure that the contract is in compliance with the laws in the country where the project is being performed.
To ensure that the contract is in compliance with the organization's procurements regulations and policies.

text: The procurement claims are monitored and managed throughout the contract life cycle in accordance with:
explanation: Procurement claims (or disputes or appeals) are documented, processed, monitored, and managed throughout the contract life cycle, usually in accordance with the terms of the contract. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498

choices.text: A. The procurement management plan
B. The procurement statement of work
C. The contracts terms and conditions
D. The bid documents

text: The project sponsor wants to know what will the project deliver in each release and when these releases will be completed. Which agile artifact would address his needs?
explanation: The product roadmap shows what each release will deliver (at high level) and the dates of releases. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 52

choices.text: Product backlog
Product roadmap
Agile charter
Sprint backlog

text: You are a project manager for a product development project. You have recently received a shipment for your project containing barcode scanners. The shipment contains twenty pieces of barcode scanners, while the contract states that the seller should provide twenty five pieces. You find that twenty pieces can satisfy the needs of your project. What is the best course of action?
explanation: Contracts must be formal. If there is a clause in the contract, it is binding for both parties. If there is a shipment which does not meet the requirements of the contract, it should not be received. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 75

choices.text: Send the shipment back to the seller.
Accept the shipment.
Issue a change order to change the contract specifications.
Deduct the cost difference from the next payment to the seller.

text: A project team is working on a software project in an adaptive environment. The team has completed the features of the first iteration and the team demonstrates the working product. The product owner sees the demonstration and rejects some stories. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: As the team completes the features usually in the form of user stories, the team periodically demonstrates the working product. The product owner sees the demonstration and accepts or declines stories. The declined stories should be added to the product backlog and reprioritized accordingly. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: The declined stories should be addressed through backlog grooming and may be incorporated into the next iteration.
The team should work immediately on the declined stories and hold another demonstration.
The product owner should follow the change control process.
The team should refer the product owner to the definition of done.

text: Frank is managing a major mechanical project using a predictive approach. The customer requested a change to the schedule baseline. Frank has determined the effect on the scope and cost, performed integrated change control, and adjusted the project baselines, the project management plan, and project documents. What should he do next?
explanation: The change has already been evaluated and approved; the next step is to inform stakeholders affected by the change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Look for impacts to quality.
Submit the change to the Change Control Board for approval.
Look for other changes from the stakeholders.
Notify stakeholders affected by the change.

text: The human resources department in an industrial company has just sent an email to all employees, just to inform them that next Monday is a vacation to all employees because of the country's national day. This is an example of:
explanation: Push communication is sent or distributed directly to specific recipients who need to receive the information. This ensures that the information is distributed but does not ensure that it actually reached or was understood by the intended audience. Push communications artifacts include letters, memos, reports, emails, faxes, voice mails, blogs, and press releases. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th

choices.text: Interactive communications
Push communications
Pull communications
Verbal communications

text: There was a meeting between IT manager and spare parts manager to discuss a major scope change. Your boss is the IT manager. After the meeting your manager contacts you to complete the paper work needed to make the change. This is an example of:
explanation: Here you are a project expediter, because simply you implement what others decided. You didn't evaluate the change or its impacts. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Management attention to scope management
A project expediter position
A change control system
A configuration management system

text: An organization is performing agile transformation. They are checking which of the current projects can be performed using agile approach. Which of the following is the best answer?
explanation: Agile projects are suitable when the team is co-located and when the team can work together. The attributes of successful agile teams include colocotion which leads to better communication, knowledge sharing and improving team dynamics. High levels of ambiguity and complexity exist in chaotic environments which need to stabilize first. Agile projects are not suitable for chaotic environments. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 40

choices.text: The projects which have high level of complexity and ambiguity.
The projects which don't require comprehensive documentation.
The projects which can be performed using virtual teams and high level of uncertainty.
The projects which have co-located team and the project team can work effectively together.

text: Mike is a project manager for a software project. He is managing a project team which consists of twelve team members. He has noticed that two of his team members are demotivated, Dave who likes relatedness and Noel who likes new challenges. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: A project team member who is motivated by challenge will respond well to stretch goals and problems to solve. A project team member who is motivated by relatedness will respond to being part of a dynamic working group. Tailoring motivation methods based on individual preferences helps to elicit the best individual and project team performance. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 25

choices.text: Mike should let both of them establish their own ways of working.
Mike should apply a motivation method for the team as a whole not per individual.
Mike should assign Dave to be a part of a dynamic group and stretch goals for Noel.
Mike should ask for a motivational bonus for both of them.

text: Mark is a project manager for a tele-communications project in his organization. Tom is his sponsor and he is working with David, the risk manager in the organization to determine the effectiveness of the risk responses. Tom and David are concerned that some of the risks assigned to Frederick, a project team member to plan for risk responses may not be addresses to the depth they would like. In this scenario, what is the role of Frederick regarding risk management?
explanation: Frederick here is a risk response owner who is responsible to plan a risk response. He is also a team member, but the question asks what is his role regarding risk management. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 426

choices.text: Risk auditor
Risk response owner
Risk team member
Project team member

text: Charles is the risk manager in the organization and at the same time he is the project sponsor for the new project. Laurence is the project manager of this project. In a critical risk, Laurence reaches out to Charles for guidance. This request is considered as:
explanation: Laurence consulted Charles because of his expertise in risk management. So, it is considered as expert judgement. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 369

choices.text: Project Communications
Project Reporting
Risk Identification
Expert Judgement

text: You are managing a product development project. During the project, the customer told you that there are three work packages, he thinks that they are not necessary. The customer doesn't have technical understanding of the project work and he wants to remove these work packages from the project scope. You can't remove these work packages, because if they are removed, the project scope will be negatively impacted. You requested a meeting with the customer and made a presentation about the importance of these work packages. Which process did you undergo?
explanation: A presentation is the formal delivery of information and/or documentation. It can include specific information aimed at increasing understanding and support of the work and objectives of the project. Presentation is a communication skill which can be used in the Manage Communications process. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 384

choices.text: Control Scope
Validate Scope
Perform Integrated Change Control
Manage Communications

text: A project team has been assigned to perform a product development project. The project team members have never worked on this type of projects before and they lack understanding and awareness about the product of the project. This project is characterized as:
explanation: Uncertainty is a lack of understanding and awareness of issues, events, paths to follow, or solutions to pursue. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 117

choices.text: High Volatility
High Uncertainty
High Ambiguity
High Complexity

text: During executing a major telecommunication project, a hardware seller has released a major upgrade for some of the equipment being installed. The upgrade would provide the customer with functionality they requested at the beginning of the project, which was not available at that time. What should you do?
explanation: As a part of professional and social responsibility, you should care about the customer's interests, so you should tell him about the available upgrades, so ignoring the upgrade and continuing as planned is not the best solution. Implementing the change can't be done unless a formal change request is submitted and approved by the CCB. Implementing the change on the remaining installations can't be done unless a formal change request is submitted and approved by the CCB. So the best answer is to inform the customer about the available upgrade and its impacts on schedule and performance. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Implement the change and adjust the schedule as necessary because this supports the customer’s original request.
Inform the customer of the available upgrade and the impacts to the project’s timeline and functionality if the upgrade is implemented.
Continue as planned. Your customer didn’t request a change.
Implement the change on the remaining installations and continue with the schedule.

text: Bill is managing an industrial project. He requested from his team to organize potential causes of quality problems into groups to know which causes they should focus on. Which tool should the team use in this situation?
explanation: Affinity diagrams can organize potential causes of defects into groups showing areas that should be focused on the most. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 293 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 178

choices.text: Control Charts
Scatter diagrams
Affinity diagrams
Why-why diagram

text: Steve is managing a large construction project which is budgeted at $20,000,000 and will be completed in 2 years. The project is on track with no major issues. Recently, the city council has announced for some new regulations related to the construction projects in the city that will take effect within two years. The new regulations will have an effect on the project budget, however there is low probability that the new regulations will take effect before the project delivery date. What should Steve do?
explanation: First things first, he together with the team should identify a risk, add it to the risk register, make needed analysis and plan a response. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Hold a meeting with the project team to discuss this topic.
It is an unknown-unknown, he can address it through management reserve.
Calculate the effect on the project budget and submit a change request to add it as contingency reserve.
Add a risk to the risk register.

text: Mark is a project manager for an industrial project. He is involved in a fixed-price (FP) contract with a supplier, which of the following might not be an output of the Control Procurement process?
explanation: Resource change approvals are not needed in fixed price contracts. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 471 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: A. Resource change approvals
B. Payment requests
C. Change requests
D. Lessons learned register updates

text: A product development project has been initiated in XYZ organization, where organization's senior management, project sponsor and project manager attempt to exert project influence in multiple, misaligned directions. Which of the following is most likely to happen?
explanation: More conflict and confusion can emerge when too many participants attempt to exert project influence in multiple, misaligned directions. Domain = People. The Standard for Project Management, Page 41

choices.text: More motivation for project team and stakeholders.
More conflict and confusion can emerge.
More alignment toward project goal.
More commitment to senior management, sponsor and project manager directions.

text: Eric is a project manager of a construction project to install new glass windows in an office building. He planned to spend $21,300 on the project, but his costs are going higher than planned, and now he is afraid that his project is spending too much money. Which of the following parameters can he use to determine whether his project is over or under budget?
explanation: Cost variance (CV) is the amount of budget deficit or surplus at a given point in time, expressed as the difference between earned value and the actual cost. It is a measure of cost performance on a project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 262 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: AC
EV
CV
VAC

text: A project manager who is managing a project using plan-driven approach has incorporated some agile practices to his project. Due to adopting this hybrid approach, the team members complain that they have to update both the plan-driven tools like Gantt chart, requirements documentation, activity attributes,..etc. and the agile tools as well like Kanban board, burndown chart, burnup chart,.etc. They feel that the new hybrid approach has added much workload on them. Which of the following should the project manager do?
explanation: The intent of using tools is to assist the teams, stakeholders, and project organizations in managing their data and methodology. So, they need to determine which tools give them the needed insights and stop using other tools. Domain = Process.

choices.text: Follow the Agile Manifesto value
Take a step back and stop agile transformation.
He needs to determine which tools provide the needed project insights.
Ask them to continue using both traditional and agile artifacts.

text: An organization has started a project to move a data center from one city to another one. The project team decides to adopt predictive approach for the physical building construction of the new data center, use iterative approach for establishing the computing and network capabilities and use a predictive approach for network migration and shutting down the old network. Which of the following statements best describes this situation?
explanation: Deciding on a life cycle and the phases of the life cycle is an example of tailoring. Additional tailoring can be done when selecting the development and delivery approach for the project. Some large projects may use a combination of development and delivery approaches simultaneously. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 134

choices.text: It is one project, the project team should have adopted one development approach.
It is not the project team decision to select the project development approach.
The project team are managing a program.
The project team tailors the life cycle and development approach.

text: All of the following statements describe the focus of the Plan Procurement Management process, except:
explanation: The focus of procurement management is to acquire goods and services from outside the project only. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 466

choices.text: Whether to acquire goods and services from outside the project.
What goods and services will be acquired from outside the project.
How and when to acquire goods and services from outside the project.
Whether to acquire goods and services from inside and outside the project, what to acquire, as well as how and when to acquire them.

text: Which of the following is the correct order from lowest level to highest level?
explanation: A user story is divided into a group of tasks. An iteration is a group of user stories. A release is a group of iterations. So the correct order from lowest level to highest level is task - user story - iteration - release. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 216

choices.text: Task - User Story - Iteration - Release
User Story - Task - Iteration - Release
Task - User Story - Release - Iteration
Task - Iteration - User Story - Release

text: Your organization is a bidder in a new office building project and you are assigned to prepare the bidding documents for this project. Your organization will provide furniture for the buyer. You need to know more details about the specifications of the needed furniture because the statement of work which the buyer sent is not detailed enough. Which document will you send to the buyer in this situation?
explanation: An RFI is used when more information on the goods and services to be acquired is needed from the sellers. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 477 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 192

choices.text: Procurement statement of work (SOW)
Request for proposal (RFP)
Request for quotation (RFQ)
Request for information (RFI)

text: Which of the following are included in the responsibility assignment matrix (RAM)?
explanation: A responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) is a grid that shows the project resources assigned to each activity or work package. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 317 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

choices.text: Activity and resource
Resource and time estimate
Activity and attributes
Activity and calendar date

text: What is the purpose of effective communication?
explanation: Communication occur throughout the project, not only during project executing. Project information should be provided in the correct format not just in writing. Information should be provided at the right time not as early as possible and with the intended impact. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 379

choices.text: Information is distributed in project executing, in the requested format, to the right recipients.
Information is in the right format, sent as early as possible, with the intended impact.
Information is provided, in writing, at the right time, to the right recipients.
Information is provided in the right format, at the right time, with the intended impact.

text: Jenny is the project manager for an electronic boards design project in a multi-national organization. She needs a supplier to provide the electronic components needed for her project. A contract should be signed with the supplier to provide the needed components on time with the required specifications. Typically, who should sign the contract with the supplier?
explanation: The project manager is typically not authorized to sign legal agreements binding the organization; this is reserved for those who have the authority to do so (the procurement manager). Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 460

choices.text: Project manager
Procurement manager
Project sponsor
Both the project manager and the procurement manager

text: Catherine is a procurement specialist. She always prefers to work in a centralized contracting environment than working in a decentralized contracting environment. Which of the following statements best describes the reason behind this?
explanation: In centralized contracting environment, there is a dedicated procurement department, but in decentralized contracting environment there is a procurement specialist for each project. In centralized contracting environment, the procurement specialist will have access to others with similar expertise so she will gain more experience. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 462

choices.text: Because in a centralized contracting environment she will gain more experience.
Because in a centralized contracting environment she will have more loyalty to the project than in a decentralized contracting.
Because in a centralized contracting environment she will report directly to the project manager.
Because startup companies use centralized contracting and the opportunity to grow in these companies is high.

text: A project is being implemented as part of a large program. During project executing, the program manager informs the project manager that due to an organizational change, his project will be terminated. The project manager wants to inform the project stakeholders and project team about project closure. Which document should he refer to?
explanation: An effective communications management plan recognizes the diverse information needs of the project’s stakeholders. It includes stakeholder communication requirements, information to be communicated, including language, format, content, and level of detail..etc. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Communication Management Plan
Stakeholder Engagement Plan
Project Closure Guidelines
Change Management Plan

text: You signed a contract with a supplier to deliver raw materials for your project. He always delivers these materials later than the due date. He asked you to accept late deliverables and in exchange he will decrease the material cost. This is an example of:
explanation: In this situation, neither parties get everything. Each party has to give up something, so it is an example for compromising. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 349 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

choices.text: Compromising
Smoothing
Forcing
Confronting

text: The hardware design phase has reached its end. This phase is a part of a three-phase sequential project. All of the following actions should be performed before announcing that the design phase has been formally closed except:
explanation: Making sure that resources are available for the next phase is a part of the next phase work not the current phase. All other choices must be done before formally closing the phase. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Making sure that project knowledge has been shared and transferred for using in future projects.
Measuring project success or failure according to the success criteria.
Making sure that resources are available for the next phase.
Finalizing all open claims with the contracted sellers in this phase.

text: Michael is managing a software project which is a part of a program. During risk identification, a team member has identified an opportunity that he will learn a different programming language to perform a part of this project which may be useful for other parts. Michael noticed that this opportunity will be useful for other projects in the program, but it will not be useful in his project. So, he informed the program manager about this opportunity. Which risk response strategy did Michael use in this situation?
explanation: In this situation, Escalate is the correct answer because the project manager agrees that the proposed response would exceed the project manager’s authority. He escalated the opportunity to be managed at the program level. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 444 and PMBOK 7th, Page 125

choices.text: Transfer, because he transferred it to the program manager.
Escalate, because it needs to be managed at the program level.
Accept, because he did nothing towards this opportunity.
Enhance, because by informing program manager probability will increase.

text: Fredric is a project manager in a multinational company. He is having a phone call with Fernando, a team member working on his project. The English accent of Fernando is very difficult to be understood by Fredric. Fernando's accent is considered as:
explanation: Language barrier is considered to be a noise, which includes any interference or barriers that might compromise the delivery of the message. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 373

choices.text: Noise
Medium
Feedback
Encoding

text: After analyzing the status of your project, you determine the earned value (EV) = 500,000 $ and the planned value (PV) = 550,000 $. What should you expect at the end of the project as a result if this trend continues?
explanation: Since EV is less than PV, therefore the amount of work performed is less than what was planned, so the project is behind schedule and will finish later than the planned date. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: The project will finish below the original cost estimate.
The actual cost will be lower than planned.
The estimate at completion will be lower than planned.
The project will finish behind schedule.

text: All of the following are among the value delivery office (VDO) roles, except:
explanation: The VDO serves an enabling role, rather than a management or oversight function. It focuses on coaching project teams; building adaptive skills and capabilities throughout the organization; and mentoring sponsors and product owners to be more effective in those roles. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 141

choices.text: Providing policies, templates and lessons learned for managing projects within the organization.
Mentoring sponsors and product owners to be more effective in those roles.
Coaching project teams.
Building adaptive skills and capabilities throughout the organization.

text: Resource management plan is concerned with planning for all the following resources except:
explanation: Monetary resources is addressed through the Estimate Costs process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 313 and 240

choices.text: Facilities
Monetary resources
Team members
Supplies

text: Preliminary risk responses may be identified and recorded as part of the ______ process and will be reviewed and confirmed as part of the ______ process.
explanation: Preliminary risk responses may be identified and recorded as part of the Identify Risks process and will be reviewed and confirmed as part of the Plan Risk Responses process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411

choices.text: Identify Risks and Plan Risk Responses
Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis and Plan Risk Responses
Identify Risks and Implement Risk Responses
Plan Risk Management and Plan Risk Responses

text: The project team has created the first project deliverable. It has been inspected and passed all the quality control tests. The project team needs to have this deliverable accepted by the customer. To obtain the deliverable acceptance, the project team might need all of the following, except:
explanation: The project team can get the deliverable acceptance through the Validate Scope process. The responsibility assignment matrix is not needed in the Validate Scope process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 165

choices.text: The project management plan, containing the scope baseline and the scope management plan.
The verified deliverables completed and checked for correctness by the Control Quality process.
The requirements traceability matrix, linking requirements to the project objectives
The responsibility assignment matrix describing the roles and responsibilities of the project team members

text: You are managing a construction project that will cost 2,000,000$ and will last for 14 months. Before finalizing the project management plan, the project sponsor asks you why you scheduled many risk identification meetings throughout the project. He said that risk identification meeting should be scheduled only during project planning and there is no need for all these risk identification meetings throughout the project. How should you answer him?
explanation: Identify risks is an iterative process, because new risks may evolve or become known as the project progresses through its life cycle. The frequency of iteration and participation in each cycle will vary by situation. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: The sponsor is your boss. You should follow his advice and schedule risk identification meetings during project planning only.
The iterative meetings allow the risk identification participants to identify newly discovered risks throughout the project.
The Identify Risks process occurs during planning process group, so the risk identification meetings should be also scheduled during planning.
The iterative meetings allow the project manager to discuss the risk events which have passed the project and which did not happen.

text: Bruce is a project manager for an industrial project. This is the first project he manages. He expects to face some difficulties with team members and stakeholders because he usually accepts others for what and who they are and always shows open concern. He is worried that his personality characteristics may affect managing the team members and achieving project objectives. What is his personality trait?
explanation: Authentic personality accepts others for what and who they are, show open concern. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 66

choices.text: Authentic
Courteous
Emotional
Social

text: A timeboxed event means that:
explanation: Timeboxed events have a maximum duration. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 154

choices.text: The event must happen at a given time.
The event must take a minimum amount of time.
The event can't take more than a maximum amount of time.
The event should take average time plus or minus 10%.

text: You are managing a large construction project which involves four contracts with four different sellers. You are about to finish your project, so you are preparing to close all the contracts and trying to settle all disputes. A dispute with one seller can't be settled by negotiation. What should you do?
explanation: If settlement cannot be achieved through direct negotiation, some form of alternative dispute resolution (ADR). Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498

choices.text: It should be handled in accordance with alternative dispute resolution.
The issues should be documented in the issue log and marked as unresolved.
The contract should be terminated according to the terms and conditions.
You should escalate the issue to the project sponsor.

text: What is the first activity done in Test Driven Development (TDD)?
explanation: Test-Driven Development (TDD) or Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) involves writing automated tests before writing/creating the product actually helps people design and mistake-proof the product. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 56

choices.text: Refactor
Write Code
Write Test
Perform a spike

text: An agile team is working on a project to develop a product for a client. The product is relatively new and the team did not work on similar product like this before. The team needs to investigate a new approach to develop the product. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: A spike is a short effort (usually timeboxed) that is devoted to exploring an approach. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 56

choices.text: Conduct a spike
Test-Driven Development (TDD)
Behavior-Driven Development
Refactoring

text: After a great deal of effort, finally, the project manager has made a change to the scope baseline. What should he do next?
explanation: As the change is already made, this means that the impacts were evaluated, the change was submitted to CCB and approved. The remaining action is to update the project management plan and project documents to reflect the change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Ask the sponsor to approve the change.
Evaluate impacts on other constraints like time, cost, and resources.
Revise the project management plan and project documents.
Send the change request to the Change Control Board.

text: Which of the following best describes the output of the Identify Risks process?
explanation: A list of prioritized risks is an output of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. The outputs of the Identify Risks process is a risk report and a risk register containing a list of risks, root causes of risks, a list of potential responses and a list of potential risk owners. So the best answer is an understanding of project risks not just a list of risks or a list of potential risk owners. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 417

choices.text: A list of potential risk owners
A list of risks
An understanding of the project risks
A list of prioritized risks

text: The daily standup usually takes more than 15 minutes because the product owner takes some time distributing tasks to the team members. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: The team is self-managed and self-organized. So, tasks are self-assigned by the team. The daily standup is for the team and by the team. There is no need for the product owner to attend the daily standup. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 53

choices.text: The scrum master should distribute tasks to team member after the daily standup.
Extend the daily standup to 30 minutes.
The scrum master should distribute tasks to team member before the daily standup.
The scrum master should ask the product owner not to attend the daily standup and not to distribute tasks to team members.

text: Richard is a product owner for an agile project. It is known that he is very pushy because he always pushes the team to accept more work during each iteration. This will lead to the team burnout. Which of the following represents the best course of action?
explanation: The best solution that the scrum master/agile coach should explain to the product owner the risk of the team burnout. Adding resources is not a proactive solution. Padding the estimates is not a professional solution. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 9

choices.text: The team should escalate the product owner to the project sponsor.
The team should pad their estimates with extra buffers.
The scrum master should explain to the product owner the risk of the team burnout.
More team members should be added to the project team.
